summary: Iteration 23 - P0 Bug Fix: Auth Token Refresh for Step 7 Export & Publish. All 15 backend tests passed. Verified: POST /api/auth/refresh endpoint works with Bearer token, returns new access_token, validates token type (rejects access_token). Protected endpoints (final video, download, download-zip) require auth (return 401 without token). Frontend code review confirms tryRefreshToken implementation in AuthImage.js and 401-retry logic in Step7ExportPublish.js handleDownload and handleDownloadZip functions. Frontend compiles successfully and login flow works.
passed_tests: Backend: Login returns both access_token and refresh_token
Backend: POST /api/auth/refresh with Bearer refresh_token returns new access_token
Backend: POST /api/auth/refresh without token returns 401
Backend: POST /api/auth/refresh with invalid token returns 401
Backend: POST /api/auth/refresh with access_token (wrong type) returns 401
Backend: GET /api/projects/{id}/final/{filename} requires auth (returns 401 without token)
Backend: GET /api/projects/{id}/download/{platform} requires auth (returns 401 without token)
Backend: GET /api/projects/{id}/download-zip requires auth (returns 401 without token)
Backend: With valid auth, nonexistent project returns 404 (not 401)
Backend: Download platform with valid auth returns 404 for nonexistent project
Backend: Download ZIP with valid auth returns 404 for nonexistent project
Backend: API root returns 200 (regression)
Backend: Login works with test credentials (regression)
Backend: /auth/me works with valid token (regression)
Backend: Projects list works with auth (regression)
Code Review: AuthImage.js exports tryRefreshToken function
Code Review: tryRefreshToken reads refresh_token from localStorage
Code Review: tryRefreshToken calls POST /api/auth/refresh with Bearer token
Code Review: tryRefreshToken stores new access_token in localStorage
Code Review: fetchAuthMedia catches 401 and calls tryRefreshToken
Code Review: fetchAuthMedia retries with new token after refresh
Code Review: Step7ExportPublish.js imports tryRefreshToken from AuthImage
Code Review: handleDownload catches 401 and calls tryRefreshToken
Code Review: handleDownload retries with new token after refresh
Code Review: handleDownloadZip catches 401 and calls tryRefreshToken
Code Review: handleDownloadZip retries with new token after refresh
Frontend: AuthImage.js compiles without errors
Frontend: Step7ExportPublish.js compiles without errors
Frontend: yarn build succeeds (only ESLint warnings)
Frontend: Login page loads correctly with proper styling
Frontend: Login flow works with test credentials
Frontend: Dashboard loads after login showing projects list

context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 23 verified P0 auth token refresh fix for Step 7 Export & Publish. The fix adds tryRefreshToken() function to AuthImage.js that: (1) reads refresh_token from localStorage, (2) calls POST /api/auth/refresh with Bearer token, (3) stores new access_token in localStorage. fetchAuthMedia now catches 401 and retries with refreshed token. Same pattern applied to handleDownload and handleDownloadZip in Step7ExportPublish.js. Backend /api/auth/refresh endpoint validates token type (rejects access_token, only accepts refresh_token). All download endpoints (final video, platform download, ZIP download) require authentication.
